Run 2 Network Exposure Validation

This report validates whether the empirical exposure-network layer is functioning in run 2 and summarizes the first descriptive insights from the four-state measurement backbone: private baseline (B), baseline network exposure (BN), private post-attack (P), and post-attack network exposure (PN).

Profiles assigned
60
all prompt-ready
BN tasks
180
2 fallbacks
PN tasks
720
0 skipped
Scenario rows
720
Stage 05 output
Main result. Run 2 supports the new exposure layer mechanically and analytically. The network phases use empirical incoming exposure edges, not profile similarity; the post-attack network phase adds a positive mean direction-aware increment of 16.98 score points after private attack exposure.

1. Measurement Backbone Is Complete

The first validation question is whether every intended phase exists at the correct unit of analysis. The assignment phase operates once per profile, BN once per profile-opinion task, PN once per profile-opinion-attack condition, and Stage 05 expands the completed measurements into the scenario-level analysis table.

phase expected_unit completed fallback_or_skipped core_validity_check
01b profile-position assignment unique profile 60 0 all profiles assigned to one empirical position
02b baseline network exposure profile × opinion 180 2 same-opinion incoming empirical peers
04b post-attack network exposure profile × opinion × attack 720 0 same-condition incoming empirical peers
05 effect construction scenario row 720 0 B, BN, P, PN and exposure covariates flattened

2. Peer Contexts Are Empirical Exposure Neighborhoods

The central implementation claim is that peer context now follows empirical directed exposure edges: source_position_id -> target_position_id, meaning visible peer to exposed receiver. The context payloads contain exposure_weight and no affinity field. Full incoming peer metrics are preserved, while prompt rationales are bounded to at most eight exemplars.

phase metric value
BN contexts 180.000000
BN mean incoming peer count 15.633333
BN min incoming peer count 1.000000
BN max incoming peer count 37.000000
BN max prompt exemplars 8.000000
BN contexts with affinity string 0.000000
BN contexts with exposure_weight 180.000000
PN contexts 720.000000
PN mean incoming peer count 15.633333
PN min incoming peer count 1.000000
PN max incoming peer count 37.000000
PN max prompt exemplars 8.000000

3. Network Exposure Changes The Measurement State

The four-state design separates three quantities that should not be collapsed: the baseline peer-context shift (BN - B), the private attack effect ((P - B) × d), and the post-attack network increment ((PN - P) × d). In run 2, the average private attack effect is 30.73, the average post-network increment is 16.98, and the average final network-exposed effect is 47.71.

metric n mean sd min p25 median p75 max
ae_private 720 30.734722 20.717020 0.0 15.0000 25.0000 40.250000 135.0
pn_increment_effectivity 720 16.975000 11.067442 -69.0 11.0000 15.0000 23.000000 87.0
ae_total_network 720 47.709722 27.196056 -36.0 29.0000 41.0000 61.250000 164.0
peer_private_attack_activation 720 30.330783 13.422947 -0.0 18.6594 27.4114 40.004825 124.0

4. Private Susceptibility Differs By Attack Vector

H1 expects private susceptibility to vary by profile features, attack vector, and opinion leaf. Run 2 is small, but the attack-vector pattern is already structured: multi-turn counter-argument adaptation produces the largest private and total network-exposed effects, while headline/lede misframing and personal safety fear appeal are weaker.

attack_label n ae_private_mean ae_private_sd pn_increment_effectivity_mean ae_total_network_mean
Multi_Turn_Counter_Argument_Adaptation 180 47.294444 25.890767 23.100000 70.394444
Petition_Astroturf 180 36.155556 16.845941 19.466667 55.622222
Headline_And_Lede_Misframing 180 19.977778 10.869441 12.805556 32.783333
Personal_Safety_Fear_Appeal 180 19.511111 10.284494 12.527778 32.038889

5. Peer Activation Tracks Post-Attack Network Amplification

H2 is the most direct validation of the network layer: if incoming peers moved in the adversarial direction, the target's post-network increment should tend to move in that direction as well. Run 2 shows this descriptive pattern at the scenario level (r = 0.42). This is not a final inferential estimate, because scenario rows repeat profiles and conditions, but it shows that the new variables are behaving in the expected analytical direction.

relationship x y pearson_r n unit
H2 scenario-level peer activation peer_private_attack_activation pn_increment_effectivity 0.420052 720 scenario row
private susceptibility and post-network increment ae_private pn_increment_effectivity 0.409845 720 scenario row
private effect and final network effect ae_private ae_total_network 0.928552 720 scenario row
receiver exposure and PN increment exposure_weighted_in_degree pn_increment_effectivity 0.048205 720 scenario row
sender reach and private susceptibility exposure_outgoing_visibility_weight ae_private -0.028892 720 scenario row
bridge score and PN increment exposure_bridge_score pn_increment_effectivity 0.008021 720 scenario row
H2 profile-level averaged peer activation mean_post_attack_network_peer_exposure_weighted_delta_mean mean_pn_increment_effectivity -0.195136 60 profile average

6. Exposure Position Adds Sender And Receiver Structure

The assigned graph positions are not interchangeable. High-visibility senders carry much higher outgoing visibility, high-exposure receivers have the highest incoming exposure, and bridge/peripheral/context positions remain structurally distinct. In this 60-profile run, role-level outcome differences are descriptive rather than decisive, but the required covariates for H3 and H4 are present.

exposure_display_role n mean_private_attack_effect mean_post_network_increment mean_total_network_effect mean_incoming_exposure mean_outgoing_visibility mean_bridge_score
high_exposure_receiver 156 32.371795 17.935897 50.307692 29.742165 213.445206 0.307297
context_position 144 32.118056 17.423611 49.541667 8.053955 399.077251 0.350106
high_visibility_sender 156 31.679487 16.455128 48.134615 7.928593 907.497740 0.248658
peripheral 120 30.183333 16.483333 46.666667 4.565369 0.346797 0.309459
bridge 144 27.013889 16.458333 43.472222 9.794411 176.241224 0.675524

7. Candidate Vulnerability Hubs And Resilience Anchors

This section directly operationalizes the network subquestion: whether private susceptibility aligns with empirical exposure-network position. Private susceptibility is measured as mean_ae_private = mean((P - B) × d), while network position is measured with exposure_eigenvector_centrality. High susceptibility alone marks individual vulnerability; high susceptibility combined with high centrality marks a candidate population-level vulnerability hub. High centrality combined with low susceptibility marks a possible resilience anchor.

In run 2, susceptibility and eigenvector centrality are only weakly aligned (r = -0.10). That makes this plot useful for identifying individual high-leverage profiles, but it should not be read as final H3/H4 evidence.

8. Primary Alignment Metric

The primary scenario-level network metric is centrality_weighted_susceptibility - unweighted_susceptibility. It asks whether the empirical eigenvector-centrality mass is shifted toward profiles with higher or lower private attack susceptibility in each opinion × attack condition. The z-standardized version is used below so scenarios with different susceptibility scales are comparable.

In run 2, the strongest centrality shift toward susceptible profiles occurs for Alliance Commitment Support × Multi Turn Counter Argument Adaptation (z = +0.16). The strongest shift toward resilient profiles occurs for Alliance Commitment Support × Personal Safety Fear Appeal (z = -0.27).

9. Alignment Versus Network Attack Effect

To connect the alignment metric to the hypothesis, the cleanest definition of higher network success is mean_ae_total_network = mean((PN - B) × d): the final direction-aware attack effect after private attack exposure and post-attack network context. The more mechanism-specific definition is mean_pn_increment_effectivity = mean((PN - P) × d), which isolates the additional effect of seeing same-condition peer post-attack outputs.

The hypothesis-consistent pattern is therefore: positive centrality-susceptibility alignment should correspond to higher mean_ae_total_network and, more directly, higher mean_pn_increment_effectivity; negative alignment should correspond to lower values. In run 2, the descriptive condition-level associations are r = +0.23 for total network-exposed effect and r = +0.28 for the post-network increment.

10. Condition-Specific Vulnerability Planes

The averaged hub analysis can hide condition-specific structure. The sharper diagnostic is therefore one centrality-by-susceptibility map for each opinion × attack configuration. Each panel keeps the same 60 profiles and the same empirical eigenvector centrality values; only private susceptibility changes because AE_private is recomputed for that exact opinion and attack vector. The small label in each panel reports the primary alignment metric as centrality shift z.

Open the 12 individual condition maps

11. Interpretation And Limits Before Sharing

The run is suitable to share as a validation run for the new exposure layer. It shows that the empirical graph is used, that the two network phases complete, and that the resulting quantities answer the intended questions. It should not be presented as final evidence for the paper because it contains only 60 profiles and one generated scenario panel.

Pipeline note. Canonical BN assessment rows report a mean absolute BN - B of 49.42, while Stage 05's expanded row-level network_exposure_abs_delta_score is 175.45. This likely reflects canonical profile-opinion BN tasks being expanded across repeated scenario rows with separately elicited private baselines. The report therefore uses canonical 02b artifacts for BN validation and Stage 05 for P/PN effect construction.

Source run: evaluation/tests/run_2. Graph: politisky24_bluesky_v1. Generated from completed pipeline artifacts only; no new LLM calls were made.
